lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Toons Universe Codes
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Use this module as your quick code desk: copy the active code, redeem it before queueing, and track where new drops are posted.

steps.title: Redeem the active code first
steps.description: Current code: THANKYOU. Redeem it for 500 Malachor before your next run to secure early currency.

steps.title: Use the left-side redeem button
steps.description: Open the game in Roblox, tap Redeem Code on the left panel, then paste the code exactly.

steps.title: Verify rewards immediately
steps.description: After tapping Redeem, confirm your Malachor balance changed before entering the elevator queue.

steps.title: Track code channels weekly
steps.description: Monitor the Roblox page, Discord announcements, Roblox group updates, and major code trackers.

quickTips: Paste codes directly to avoid typo failures.
Redeem before shopping so you can compare unlock options immediately.
Keep expired codes in a separate list to reduce confusion for new visitors.
Update this module whenever official channels post fresh reward drops.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Toons Universe Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: The early loop is straightforward: choose a stable agent, complete machines safely, manage stamina, then reach the elevator before panic pressure ends the floor.

cards.name: Choose a safe starter
cards.description: Nimbus is a forgiving solo pick. Weffle is an easy support option for duo and group runs.

cards.name: Prioritize machines over loot
cards.description: Objective completion controls pace. Loot only when nearby routes are clear.

cards.name: Save stamina for danger windows
cards.description: Do not sprint constantly. Keep stamina reserve for line-of-sight breaks and elevator pushes.

cards.name: Coordinate before panic mode
cards.description: When panic starts, stop side tasks and call a safe route back to the elevator together.

milestones: Reach elevator during panic mode with team intact

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Toons Universe Tier List
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: This practical tier snapshot prioritizes consistency for public lobbies and early progression rather than niche high-risk setups.

items.name: S Tier: Nimbus
items.description: Free access, strong stamina sustain, and reliable recovery windows for long chases.

items.name: S Tier: Weffle
items.description: Strong speed utility for teammates and steady value in coordinated objective runs.

items.name: A Tier: Patcher / Phantasia / Copper / Selena
items.description: Excellent support and information tools with strong team payoff when coordinated.

items.name: B Tier: Discard / Koi / Squiddle / Teipo
items.description: Useful kits that perform best when positioning and route discipline are already stable.

items.name: C Tier: Guido and Rossum
items.description: Objective-focused value but demands careful pathing because movement and stamina are limited.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Toons Universe Agents List
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Use this compressed roster to decide who to unlock next based on role fit, team need, and consistency under pressure.

solutions.name: Nimbus
solutions.description: Free starter with strong stamina recovery, great for players learning chase pacing.

solutions.name: Weffle
solutions.description: Good duo/group support with movement utility that helps teammates stabilize runs.

solutions.name: Fizzle / Selena
solutions.description: Useful for tracking threats or redirecting pressure while objectives are being handled.

solutions.name: Patcher / Copper / Phantasia
solutions.description: Healing, shielding, and buff tools that increase team survival in high-pressure floors.

solutions.name: Discard / Koi / Squiddle / Teipo / Guido and Rossum
solutions.description: Role picks for faster machines, extraction boosts, and structured team comps.

managementTips: Prioritize one dependable pick before broad unlock spending.
Choose unlocks based on your run role, not only novelty.
Pair low-survivability picks with support teammates.
Re-evaluate priorities when major balance updates land.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Toons Universe Agent Unlock Requirements
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: Unlock planning is easier when you separate immediate value picks from longer requirement chains tied to floor events and ability usage.

cards.name: Free Entry Picks
cards.description: Nimbus and Weffle are available immediately and anchor most early progression paths.

cards.name: Store-Based Unlocks
cards.description: Several agents are tied to Chekhov's Store and should be timed around your Malachor reserve.

cards.name: Requirement Chains
cards.description: Some unlocks need event survival, ability usage, or objective milestones beyond raw currency cost.

cards.name: Practical Sequence
cards.description: Unlock one stable support option first, then branch into specialist picks for your main playstyle.

highlights: Track Malachor cost and task prerequisites together
Avoid overbuying before a stable core roster is built
Plan unlocks around your team role
Use code rewards to accelerate first priority unlocks

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Toons Universe Stats, Sanity, and Stamina Guide
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Understanding stat tradeoffs is the fastest way to pick the right agent for your floor role and lobby difficulty.

regions.name: Speed
regions.description: Higher speed helps route pivots and chase breaks, especially when corridors are contested.

regions.name: Stamina
regions.description: Stamina determines how long you can sustain escapes and recover from sudden threat spikes.

regions.name: Sanity
regions.description: Sanity influences pressure tolerance; low sanity picks require cleaner positioning discipline.

regions.name: Hacking and Extracting
regions.description: These stats directly impact machine efficiency and should match your planned team role.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Toons Universe Malachor Farming
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Strong farming is about consistent clears and safe extraction, not risky greed during active threat windows.

creatures.name: Machine Completion
creatures.description: Steady machine progress plus successful floor exits forms the most reliable currency pipeline.

creatures.name: Code Redeems
creatures.description: Use active codes early to fund first unlocks without delaying progression.

creatures.name: Capsule Collection
creatures.description: Collect capsules on safe routes to support research goals while preserving floor tempo.

creatures.name: Controlled Spending
creatures.description: Reserve currency for role-critical unlocks before cosmetic or low-impact purchases.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Toons Universe Infected Guide
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: Infected pressure is manageable with clean route calls, stamina discipline, and role-based response timing.

items.name: Line-of-Sight Breaks
items.description: Corners and room transitions are your safest tools for reducing chase pressure.

items.name: Team Spacing
items.description: Stay close enough for support, but avoid stacking so one threat path hits everyone.

items.name: Ability Timing
items.description: Use shields, heals, dashes, or distraction tools before stamina collapse, not after.

items.name: Panic Routing
items.description: During panic mode, commit to the safest shared route and avoid side loot detours.

unlockMilestones: Identify threat routes early each floor
Call safe regroup points after machine spikes
Reach elevator with coordinated panic rotations

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Toons Universe Machines and Skill Checks
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Objective execution decides run pace. Teams that coordinate machine assignments and protect operators clear deeper floors more consistently.

sections.name: Machine Priority
sections.description: Assign high extracting or hacking agents to primary objective lanes first.

sections.name: Skill Check Discipline
sections.description: Avoid panic inputs. Controlled timing prevents costly failures and extra danger pressure.

sections.name: Safe Split Strategy
sections.description: Split only when routes are known and support can rotate quickly to a chased teammate.

sections.name: Panic Transition
sections.description: As final machines fill, shift immediately from objective mode to elevator return mode.

growthMilestones: Reduce failed check rate in public lobbies
Complete objectives with stable support rotations
Exit panic mode floors without isolated eliminations

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Toons Universe Floors and Elevator Guide
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: Think of each floor as a loop: entry setup, machine progress, controlled looting, and elevator extraction under panic pressure.

priorities.name: Lobby Setup
priorities.description: Confirm team roles and starter route intent before entering the elevator.

priorities.name: Early Floor Routing
priorities.description: Secure nearest objectives first to avoid scattered long-distance recoveries.

priorities.name: Mid-Floor Tempo
priorities.description: Balance objective speed with safe item pickup only when infected paths are clear.

priorities.name: Panic Return
priorities.description: Once panic mode starts, full team focus shifts to elevator extraction without delay.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Toons Universe Floor Events Guide
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: Event floors punish hesitation. Treat each event as a specific playbook with known risks and response priorities.

achievements.title: Blackout
achievements.description: Regroup quickly, reduce split distance, and rely on callouts over risky solo routing.

achievements.title: Fog Pressure
achievements.description: Slow movement decisions and avoid blind corner commitments without teammate support.

achievements.title: Gas Leak
achievements.description: Shorten objective greed windows and preserve stamina for forced route adjustments.

achievements.title: High Threat Spawn
achievements.description: Rotate support utility early so objective players are not isolated.

achievements.title: Elevator Breakdown
achievements.description: Expect delayed extraction timing and maintain conservative positioning until safe path opens.

achievements.title: Objective Re-route
achievements.description: Reassign roles fast when normal machine paths are blocked by event changes.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Toons Universe Cards and Voting Guide
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Card votes shape the next floor. Pick options that improve team survival and objective tempo, not only individual preference.

faqs.question: When should we vote for economy cards?
faqs.answer: Choose economy when your team is stable and can convert extra resources into immediate run value.

faqs.question: When should we prioritize safety cards?
faqs.answer: Pick safety-focused cards when public lobby coordination is weak or recent floors had repeated chase collapses.

faqs.question: Are objective-speed cards always best?
faqs.answer: They are strong when your team can protect machine operators; otherwise speed without safety often backfires.

faqs.question: How should we break tie votes?
faqs.answer: Use the card that supports the weakest role in your current team comp rather than the strongest role.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Toons Universe Items and Shops Guide
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: Shop value depends on floor state. Buy items that solve the next likely failure point instead of spending by habit.

faqs.question: What is the first shopping priority?
faqs.answer: Prioritize items that improve survival consistency, then objective speed, then optional utility.

faqs.question: Should we spend all currency each stop?
faqs.answer: No. Keep reserve for event-heavy floors and role-critical opportunities that appear later.

faqs.question: Who should receive support items first?
faqs.answer: Give protection and sustain tools to players who hold objectives under the highest risk.

faqs.question: When should we skip purchases?
faqs.answer: Skip low-impact options when your team already has adequate utility for the current floor plan.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Toons Universe Research, Trinkets, and Emblems
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: Long-term account growth comes from consistent capsule routes, focused research goals, and trinket/emblem choices that match your role.

settings.name: Research Capsules
settings.description: Collect capsules on safe paths to advance infected research without sacrificing floor stability.

settings.name: Trinket Selection
settings.description: Choose trinkets that reinforce your core job: survival, support, or objective throughput.

settings.name: Emblem Milestones
settings.description: Track emblem milestones and prioritize those that improve consistent clear rates.

settings.name: Role Alignment
settings.description: Avoid mixed-purpose loadouts; focused builds usually outperform unfocused hybrids.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: This timeline section tracks launch context, official media milestones, and update categories that affect real run strategy.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Toons Universe Official Links and Community
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: Use verified destinations for announcements, community discussions, and media updates to avoid outdated third-party confusion.

steps.title: Play on the official Roblox experience
Follow Toon Shenanigans channels
steps.description: Track official videos and teasers on the Toon Shenanigans YouTube channel.

steps.title: Check community references carefully
steps.description: Use community wikis and social threads for discovery, then confirm major changes via official channels.

faq.title: Toons Universe
faq.subtitle: Quick answers for codes, progression, and run strategy

questions.question: How often do Toons Universe codes change?
questions.answer: Code drops are irregular, so checking official channels and update trackers weekly is the most reliable routine.

questions.question: Which starter is better for solo runs?
questions.answer: Nimbus is usually the most forgiving solo starter because stamina recovery helps recover from chase mistakes.

questions.question: What should we do when panic mode starts?
questions.answer: Stop side looting, call a safe return route, and commit the whole team to elevator extraction immediately.

questions.question: Is Malachor better spent on cosmetics or agents first?
questions.answer: Early progression is generally smoother when currency is focused on role-critical agent unlocks first.

questions.question: How do we improve public lobby clear rates?
questions.answer: Keep machine assignments simple, avoid over-splitting, and rotate support abilities before stamina collapses.

questions.question: Where can I find official updates and trailer posts?
questions.answer: Use the Roblox experience page, official Discord, and the Toon Shenanigans YouTube channel for update tracking.
